summary: Diane Hendricks describes the origin of ABC Supply, explaining how her experience as a roofing contractor exposed a critical flaw in the industry's supply chain. By recognizing the lack of a national distribution network for roofing products, she and her husband spent two years planning and testing a wholesale model before launching a business designed to give contractors the respect, selection, and convenience they had lacked.

principles.statement: Build businesses that solve the exact problems you faced as a customer.
principles.rationale: As contractors, they struggled to source specific products nationally because manufacturers only distributed locally. They built ABC Supply to provide the exact service they had needed themselves.
principles.source_quote: we did everything that we could to make the contractor have what we hadn't been provided for as contractors

principles.statement: Test your business model on a smaller scale before fully launching.
principles.rationale: Before opening their first official ABC Supply store, they practiced running a wholesale store for real estate products to learn the mechanics of distribution.
principles.source_quote: we practiced on a wholesale store that sold products for real estate people just to see what it was like and from there we decided we were ready

principles.statement: Treat your customers with respect by making their jobs easier.
principles.rationale: Contractors were underserved by the existing manufacturer-direct model, so the goal was to carry diverse products nationally to simplify the contractor's work and show them respect.
principles.source_quote: it was always to carry those products that would make it easy Across the Nation but would also we would be giving that contractor the respect that he needed

mental_models.name: Founder as Customer
mental_models.description: Looking at an industry from the perspective of the end-user to identify what the current supply chain fails to provide.
mental_models.example: Realizing that manufacturers acting as their own distributors created regional pockets, preventing contractors from getting specified products for national or military jobs.

heuristics: If you bid really low on an open contract, you're going to worry a lot about the job.

signature_quotes.text: we did everything that we could to make the contractor have what we hadn't been provided for as contractors
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the core mission behind founding ABC Supply.

signature_quotes.text: over the course of five ten years we realized that there needed to be a distribution chain to be able to offer to the customer us the contractor the selection of various manufacturing products Across the Nation
signature_quotes.context: Describing the realization of the market gap in the roofing industry.

signature_quotes.text: we practiced on a wholesale store that sold products for real estate people just to see what it was like and from there we decided we were ready
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how they tested the distribution model before launching ABC Supply.

anti_patterns.description: Manufacturers acting as the sole distributors of their own products.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates isolated regional pockets of product availability, making it impossible for contractors to source specific materials for national or out-of-area jobs.

anti_patterns.description: Bidding too low on open contracts.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It causes immense stress and worry about fulfilling the job profitably.
